name: Corpus Callosum
location: Deep central longitudinal fissure linking the left and right cerebral hemispheres.
description: A massive arching white matter tract containing over 200 million myelinated nerve fibers connecting homologous regions of the left and right cerebral hemispheres.
functions: Transfer of learned cognitive skills between hemispheres
bloodSupply: Anterior Communicating Artery and ACA pericallosal branch
clinicalImportance: Surgical transection (corpus callosotomy) is performed in severe intractable epilepsy to prevent seizure generalization across hemispheres.
researchNotes: Sperry's landmark split-brain experiments proved functional lateralization between language-dominant left and spatial right hemispheres.
interestingFacts: Contains over 200 million individual myelinated axon fibers.
Thicker in musicians who trained early in childhood.
